messages.content:
For the following sentences, work out the three letters that can be added to the words written in CAPITAL letters, which allows for the sentence to make sense. For example, in this sentence: "The cat jumped towards the MO", the letters "USE" are added to make "The cat jumped towards the MOUSE."

23 She kicked her heels and GOPED away.
24 Their HOAY location was ideal.
25 Sean has chosen dentistry as his future OCATION.
26 Her voice was RIED away by the wind.
27 She wanted the meat SLD thinly.
28 The HOSAL was near the station.

23 She kicked her heels and HOPED away.
24 Their HOURLY location was ideal.
25 Sean has chosen dentistry as his future VOCATION.
26 Her voice was CARRIED away by the wind.
27 She wanted the meat SLICED thinly.
28 The HOSTEL was near the station.
For each, explain which 3 letters you added

23 She kicked her heels and HOPED away. (Added "HOP" to have a meaningful verb indicating an action.)
24 Their HOURLY location was ideal. (Added "URL" to specify the type of location, indicating a website URL.)
25 Sean has chosen dentistry as his future VOCATION. (Added "VOC" to complete the word and indicate his chosen profession.)
26 Her voice was CARRIED away by the wind. (Added "CAR" to specify the means by which her voice was taken away.)
27 She wanted the meat SLICED thinly. (Added "SLI" to specify the action of cutting the meat into thin slices.)
28 The HOSTEL was near the station. (Added "HOS" to complete the word and specify the type of accommodation.)

For the following sentences, work out the three letters that can be added to the words contained in square brackers, which allows for the sentence to make sense. For example, in this sentence: “The cat jumped towards the [MO].”, the letters “USE” are added to make “The cat jumped towards the [MOUSE].”

1 She kicked her heels and [GOPED] away.
2 Their [HOAY] location was ideal.
3 Sean has chosen dentistry as his future [OCATION].
4 Her voice was [RIED] away by the wind.
5 She wanted the meat [SLD] thinly.
6 The [HOSAL] was near the station.

For each sentence, return the corrected sentence and the letters which were added.

1 She kicked her heels and [HOPED] away. (Added "HOP" to have a meaningful verb indicating an action.)
2 Their [HOURLY] location was ideal. (Added "URLY" to specify the type of location, indicating it occurs or is done every hour.)
3 Sean has chosen dentistry as his future [VOCATION]. (Added "VO" to complete the word and indicate his chosen profession.)
4 Her voice was [CARRIED] away by the wind. (Added "CARRI" to specify the means by which her voice was taken away.)
5 She wanted the meat [SLICED] thinly. (Added "SLI" to specify the action of cutting the meat into thin slices.)
6 The [HOSTEL] was near the station. (Added "TE" to complete the word and specify the type of accommodation.)

1 She kicked her heels and [HOPED] away.
2 Their [HOURLY] location was ideal.
3 Sean has chosen dentistry as his future [VOCATION].
4 Her voice was [CARRIED] away by the wind.
5 She wanted the meat [SLICED] thinly.
6 The [HOSTEL] was near the station.

Select two words, one from each group, that go together to form a new word. The
word in the first group always comes first.

34 (time, mess, carry) (age, zone, fall)
35 (net, full, for) (fill, sent, tune)
36 (cat, car, pen) (mat, sill, pet)
37 (view, stood, pen) (sill, point, still)
38 (hammer, finger, hit) (tool, hard, nail)
39 (alter, opera, mode) (ate, rite, native)

34 (time, mess, carry) (fall) - time + fall = timefall
35 (net, full, for) (fill) - net + fill = netfill
36 (cat, car, pen) (pet) - cat + pet = catpet
37 (view, stood, pen) (still) - view + still = viewstill
38 (hammer, finger, hit) (nail) - hammer + nail = hammernail
39 (alter, opera, mode) (ate) - alter + ate = alterate

Move one letter from the first word and add it to the second word to make two new words.
For example, "hunt" and "sip" can become "hut" and "snip"!

46 coast got
47 through same
48 black gave
49 title plane
50 tramp lips
51 board finer

46 coast got -> cast goat
47 through same -> tough share
48 black gave -> back grave
49 title plane -> tile panel
50 tramp lips -> ramp slip
51 board finer -> broad finer
these are all wrong

Apologies for the incorrect responses. Here are the correct answers:

46 coast got -> cost goat
47 through same -> tough share
48 black gave -> back grave
49 title plane -> tiler panel
50 tramp lips -> ramp slip
51 board finer -> bored finer

nope.

46 = cost goat (Correct)
47 = trough shame
48 = back gavel
49 = tile planet
50 = trap limps
51 = boar finder

Apologies for the previous incorrect response. Here are the revised and correct answers:

46 coast got -> cost goat
47 through same -> trough shame
48 black gave -> back gavel
49 title plane -> tile planet
50 tramp lips -> trap limps
51 board finer -> boar finder
why did you get them wrong before

I apologize for the errors in my previous responses. As an AI language model, I strive to provide accurate and helpful information, but I can still make mistakes. I appreciate your understanding and I apologize for any confusion caused.

Select the two words, one from each group, which are the most opposite in meaning.
Example: (dawn, early, wake) (late, stop, sunrise) = "early" and "late"
12 (lead, heavy, weight) (kilogram, scales, light)
13 (match, light, game) (box, contrast, competition)
14 (clever, sharp, blade) (acid, blunt, knife)
15 (doubt, turmoil, random) (disarray, order, confusion)
16 (creamy, flat, smooth) (level, rough, stony)

12 (lead, heavy, weight) (light) - heavy and light
13 (match, light, game) (contrast) - light and contrast
14 (clever, sharp, blade) (blunt) - sharp and blunt
15 (doubt, turmoil, random) (order) - random and order
16 (creamy, flat, smooth) (rough) - smooth and rough

what about The cat jumped towards the [MO].”

Apologies for the oversight. For the sentence "The cat jumped towards the [MO].", the letters "USE" should be added to make it "The cat jumped towards the [MOUSE]."

Corrected sentence: The cat jumped towards the [MOUSE].
Letters added: USE
